macro_summary: Rate backdrop (FRED, not live): payrolls +57k (cooling from +129k); unemployment 4.2% (down); last CPI 3.7% YoY (down). US equities closed mixed on Wednesday as a 14% five-day surge in oil prices and renewed US-Iran naval strikes kept risk-on conviction in check, even after June producer prices unexpectedly fell (per Reuters, July 15) and cooler CPI the day before eased immediate Fed rate-hike fears. Tech and semis sold off sharply — MU -8.6%, SK Hynix -12.7% — on Chinese memory-competition fears and profit-taking, while mega-cap Apple and Google masked the weakness. The FOMC decision on July 29 is the next macro binary; for now, the rates backdrop is supportive after back-to-back benign inflation prints.
commodities_note: WTI settled at $79.47/bbl, pulling back 0.8% after a 10% five-day run but held above the key $75 level as CENTCOM launched fresh strikes on Iran (per Reuters, July 15). Gold slipped 0.6% to $4,040/oz, still capped below the $4,091–4,104 resistance band. DXY at 100.52 remains subdued, and the US 10-year yield fell to 4.55% on the soft CPI/PPI double. The crypto-briefing.com oil narrative now reflects a 5.9% chance of a new all-time high by end-September, up modestly.

writeup.headline: Down 43% over month — GaN power play, but R:R is 6.8:1, distorted.
writeup.what_to_do: The GaN chip narrative is intact as AI‑power infrastructure expands, but the stock is 36% below the 50‑day; the 6.8:1 R:R is distorted by a 5% stop. Reports in 11 days — bull mechanism: AI‑datacenter GaN design wins; bear: cash burn continues. No entry until a specific design win is named. Relative strength: lagging AI Power Infrastructure by 20.9% over the month.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if NVTS Jul 27 earnings miss by >5% or guidance is cut.

The sizing R:R to $128.75 is 0.5:1 — no edge. Wait for a pullback to $128.75 support and a PE contraction toward 45x before considering entry.

thesis_highlights: ADR/China delisting risk is a standing consideration — always maintain starter sizing for WRD.

writeup.headline: Hard-blocked on momentum chase — +14.9% 5‑day, delisting risk persists.
writeup.what_to_do: The 5‑day surge violates the momentum chase block. ADR delisting risk is a standing thesis invalidator (per WRD guardrails). The R:R is 3.4:1 but unrealized because of the regulatory overhang. Revisit only if the stock consolidates below $6.03 support and the delisting threat is explicitly resolved.

SITM.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 50x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

writeup.headline: Renesas timing acquisition closed — MEMS timing thesis strengthens.
writeup.what_to_do: The Renesas timing acquisition (per Insider Monkey, July 15) tenfolds SITM's clocking portfolio, creating a clean leader in precision timing for AI datacenters. R:R headline is distorted, but sizing R:R to $564 is 3.0:1. The stock is 12.4% below the 50‑day; a bounce from $609.15 support with above‑average volume would be a half‑position entry. Fragility: high‑valuation tech — forward PE 50x, PEG 3.8 — but the acquisition provides a second leg to the thesis.
writeup.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 50x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if SITM fails to reclaim SMA50 ($698.81) on above-average volume within the next 10 sessions.

singapore.summary: The banks remain in a uniform hard‑block CAUTION as NIM compression from dual USD/SGD headwinds persists. Singapore Q2 GDP beat (per news digest) didn't change the technical picture — all three are extended with no near‑term catalyst for a pullback that resets the 50‑day. The thesis is intact for DBS's wealth‑management ambition, but entry is blocked for months.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically blocked on extreme extension
singapore.key_development: DBS targets $1T wealth assets by 2030 (per Reuters, July 15), but no change to the rate‑sensitive NIM picture.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster is in a severe selloff — MU, SK Hynix, and Samsung all fell sharply on Chinese memory‑competition fears, while ASML's guidance raise couldn't stop the rout. Nine of eleven names are below their 50‑day average, with only NVDA and AMD holding above. The cluster is now 21% below its May peak; bottom‑fishing is premature until TSMC earnings tonight provide a demand‑side read.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically oversold — awaiting TSMC demand signal
semiconductors.key_development: Micron -8.6%, SK Hynix -12.7% on China memory fears; ASML raises FY26 guidance but semis sell off broadly.

big_tech.summary: BigTech diverged sharply — Google and Amazon rallied on rotation out of semis, while Microsoft benefitted from cost‑cut reports. All four are within 2% of their 50‑day averages, but none offer clean low‑risk entry points: R:R ratios are uniformly below 1.5:1 when adjusted for stop tightness. Earnings season kicks off next week and will be the catalyst to break the stalemate.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — AI capex sustainability under scrutiny, earnings the catalyst
big_tech.key_development: Microsoft reportedly training salespeople to downplay OpenAI models (per TechCrunch, July 15).

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA $2B photonics partner; both COHR and LITE move as a basket on optics demand signals.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back to $85 and LITE holds $680 support — the pair signals photonics infrastructure buildout.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and data‑center infrastructure play; direct competitor to AVGO and beneficiary of NVDA‑to‑ASIC diversification.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after AVGO earnings on Sep 3 for a read on custom‑ASIC TAM growth; a pullback to $120 would be attractive.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: NVDA Spectrum‑X ecosystem partner; 800G/1.6T networking demand reads through to LITE and the optics trade.
interconnected.entry_note: Watch if LITE rebounds: ANET above $450 would confirm enterprise AI networking demand.

active_risks: US-Iran naval clashes intensify in the Strait of Hormuz; US strikes Iran Revolutionary Guard targets, Iran threatens to block all Mideast energy exports (per Reuters & Times of Israel, July 15).
China helium export halt persists, tightening semiconductor manufacturing gas supply (per news digest).
US tightens tech export controls on China, with only 'very few' Nvidia H200 chips shipped and new policy restricting research collaboration (per Reuters, July 14).
South Korea's central bank hikes rates for the first time since 2023 to curb inflation and debt (per AP, July 16).
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $79.47 is pressuring semis via both higher input costs and a rotation toward energy; the semis cluster (MU, 000660.KS, 005930.KS) is down sharply on memory-pricing fears compounded by China competition. Banks benefit from higher yields, but the Singapore trio are too extended to enter. The tech sector is offloading risk ahead of TSMC earnings tonight.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: US launched fresh strikes on Iran (per Times of Israel, July 15); South Korea unexpectedly hiked rates (per AP, July 16).

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds, WTI around $80; semis selloff moderates but stays below 50‑day averages. TSMC earnings tonight confirm AI demand remains strong but offer no upside surprise — NVDA, ASML drift. Singapore bank extension persists; no entry.

optimistic.description: Quick Iran ceasefire or de‑escalation; oil falls back toward $70, easing inflation fears and rate‑hike odds. Semis snap back as memory fears fade — MU, 000660.KS rally 5‑10% from support, opening WATCH entries.

pessimistic.description: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz; WTI above $90, triggering a broader equity selloff and pushing the Fed to hike. Semis break support levels; INTC, CRWV, NVTS hit new lows. AVOID dominates — cash is king.

wildcard.description: A China‑US trade deal on helium and chips is announced alongside a Hormuz ceasefire, creating a violent snap‑back rally in semis; SITM, LITE, and BE surge 15%+ on renewed AI infrastructure spending.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Stay in cash for fresh entries — the semis selloff is binary on TSMC earnings and Iran; wait for a clear risk‑off purge or a bullish demand confirmation before deploying.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 5 of 31 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, while the remaining caution is base‑scorer driven — no unsourced AVOID. The dominant risk is the Hormuz-driven oil spike squeezing both semis demand expectations and memory pricing narratives, with the semis cluster under heavy selling pressure. Cash investors should wait for TSMC earnings tonight and a resolution of the Iran supply disruption before entering new positions.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=8) / ACCUMULATE (n=9) spanning chop, trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
